rationale: All three Cynewulf works (Andreas, Elene, Juliana) are correctly identified with accurate one-sentence themes grounded in actual corpus content. Paragraphs p-868413 (Andreas ch.1) and p-222dfa (Elene ch.1) both resolve and substantiate the claims; Juliana's citation correctly references ch.1 but has null for paragraph_id, which is a minor grounding gap rather than a fabrication.
verifications_performed: Ran list_works {author:'cynewulf'} — confirmed exactly 3 works: Andreas (cynewulf-andreas-07b573), Elene (cynewulf-elene-d2d132), Juliana (cynewulf-juliana-9a2157)
Pulled paragraph p-868413 from cynewulf-andreas-07b573 ch.1 — confirmed 'twelve true thanes' opening and Mermedonian missionary context
Pulled paragraph p-222dfa from cynewulf-elene-d2d132 ch.1 — confirmed Constantine's reign and True Cross quest opening

Read cynewulf-juliana-9a2157 ch.1 — confirmed Juliana's resistance to pagan reeve Eleusius, her martyrdom, and the hagiographic theme; paragraph_id was null in citation but chapter content matches the claim
